meta.title: Zustand - Simple and Scalable State Management for React
meta.description: Learn Zustand, a small, fast and scalable state management solution for React. Complete documentation, tutorials, and examples.
meta.keywords: zustand, react state management, react hooks, state management, react tutorial, zustand tutorial

hero.title: Zustand
hero.subtitle: A small, fast and scalable bearbones state-management solution
hero.description: Zustand is a simple and performant state management library for React applications. It has a minimal API, is easy to learn, and doesn't require boilerplate code.

simple.title: Simple & Minimal
simple.description: No providers, no context, just pure React hooks. Simple API that's easy to learn and use.

fast.title: Fast & Performant
fast.description: Optimized for performance with automatic render optimization and minimal re-renders.

scalable.title: Scalable
scalable.description: Works great for small apps and scales effortlessly to large, complex applications.

typescript.title: TypeScript First
typescript.description: Built with TypeScript in mind, providing excellent type inference and developer experience.

devtools.title: DevTools Support
devtools.description: Integrate with Redux DevTools for easy debugging and state inspection.

middleware.title: Flexible Middleware
middleware.description: Extend functionality with middleware for persistence, devtools, and more.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.step3: Use the store in your components

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.description: Explore our comprehensive documentation and tutorials to master Zustand.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.installDesc: Install Zustand using npm or yarn:
gettingStarted.firstStoreDesc: Creating a store is straightforward. Here's a simple counter example:
gettingStarted.basicUsageDesc: Use the store in your React components:

coreConcepts.title: Core Concepts
coreConcepts.storeDesc: A store holds your application state. It's created using the create function and returns a React hook.
coreConcepts.stateDesc: The state is the data that your store holds. It can be any JavaScript value - objects, arrays, primitives, etc.
coreConcepts.actionsDesc: Actions are functions that modify the state. They use the set function to update the store.
coreConcepts.selectorsDesc: Selectors allow you to subscribe to specific parts of the state, optimizing re-renders.

advanced.title: Advanced Topics
advanced.middlewareDesc: Extend Zustand with middleware for logging, persistence, and more.
advanced.persistDesc: Save your state to localStorage or sessionStorage automatically.
advanced.devtoolsDesc: Debug your state with Redux DevTools integration.
advanced.immerDesc: Use Immer for easier immutable state updates.

footer.description: Learn Zustand - A comprehensive guide to state management in React

overview.title: Tutorial Overview
overview.description: Welcome to the comprehensive Zustand tutorial! This guide will take you from beginner to advanced, teaching you everything you need to know about state management with Zustand.

lesson1.title: Lesson 1: Introduction to Zustand
lesson1.intro: Zustand is a lightweight state management library for React that provides:
lesson1.whyDesc: Compared to other state management solutions like Redux or MobX, Zustand offers a simpler API with less boilerplate while maintaining powerful features and excellent performance.

lesson2.description: Let's create a simple todo list application:

examples.title: Zustand Examples
examples.description: Explore these practical examples to learn how to use Zustand in real-world applications.

counter.title: Simple Counter
counter.description: A basic counter example demonstrating state updates and actions.

cart.title: Shopping Cart
cart.description: A shopping cart with add, remove, and quantity management.

form.title: Form Management
form.description: Managing form state with validation and submission.

async.title: Async Data Fetching
async.description: Handling API calls with loading and error states.

create.title: create()
create.description: Creates a store with the given state creator function.
create.paramDesc: stateCreator - Function that receives set, get, and api
create.returnsDesc: A React hook that can be used to access the store

set.title: set()
set.description: Updates the store state. Can be called with a partial state object or an updater function.
set.paramPartial: partial - Partial state object or updater function
set.paramReplace: replace - (optional) Replace entire state instead of merging

get.title: get()
get.description: Gets the current state. Useful in actions or outside of React components.

subscribe.title: subscribe()
subscribe.description: Subscribe to state changes. Called whenever the state updates.

middleware.title: Middleware
middleware.persistDesc: Persists store state to localStorage or sessionStorage.
middleware.devtoolsDesc: Enables Redux DevTools integration for debugging.
middleware.immerDesc: Enables Immer for easier immutable updates.

selectors.title: Selectors
selectors.description: Select specific parts of state to optimize re-renders.

whatIs.title: What is Middleware?
whatIs.description: Middleware in Zustand allows you to extend and customize your store's behavior. You can add logging, persistence, devtools integration, and more.

custom.title: Creating Custom Middleware
custom.description: A middleware is a function that wraps the store and can intercept state changes:

combining.description: You can combine multiple middlewares by nesting them:

basicUsage.description: The persist middleware automatically saves your state to storage and rehydrates it on page load:

partial.description: Only persist specific parts of your state:

naming.description: For better debugging experience, name your actions:

combining.description: When combining with other middlewares, devtools should be the outermost:

production.description: Disable devtools in production for better performance:

why.title: Why Immer?
why.description: Immer allows you to write "mutable" code that produces immutable updates. This is especially useful when dealing with deeply nested state:
